title: potentilla × subquinata
intro: Potentilla × subquinata, also known as Torngreen Cinquefoil, is a hybrid plant species that is a cross between Potentilla fruticosa and Potentilla rupestris. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Potentilla × subquinata include reducing inflammation, treating wounds and skin irritations, and relieving symptoms of arthritis and other joint-related conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to treat a variety of health issues.
